nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
# 🦙 Ollama Chat Workflow

A simple N8N workflow that integrates Ollama LLM for chat message processing and returns a structured JSON object.

## Overview
This workflow creates a chat interface that processes messages using the Llama 3.2 model through Ollama. When a chat message is received, it gets processed through a basic LLM chain and returns a response.

## Components
- **Trigger Node**
- **Processing Node**
- **Model Node**
- **JSON to Object Node**
- **Structured Response Node**
- **Error Response Node**

## Workflow Structure
1. The chat trigger node receives incoming messages
2. Messages are passed to the Basic LLM Chain
3. The Ollama Model processes the input using Llama 3.2
4. Responses are returned through the chain

## Prerequisites
- N8N installation
- Ollama setup with Llama 3.2 model
- Valid Ollama API credentials

## Configuration
1. Set up the Ollama API credentials in N8N
2. Ensure the Llama 3.2 model is available in your Ollama installation

nodes.name: Sticky Note5

parameters.content:
## JSON to Object Node
- Type: Set Node
- Purpose: A node designed to transform and structure response data in a specific format before sending it through the workflow. It operates in manual mapping mode to allow precise control over the response format.

**Key Features**
- Manual field mapping capabilities
- Object transformation and restructuring
- Support for JSON data formatting
- Field-to-field value mapping
- Includes option to add additional input fields

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

- Type: Set Node
- Purpose: Controls how the workflow responds to users chat prompt.

**Response Mode**
- Manual Mapping: Allows custom formatting of response data
- Fields to Set: Specify which data fields to include in response

nodes.name: Error Response
nodes.notes: This set node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note7

parameters.content:
## Error Response Node
- Type: Set Node
- Purpose: Handles error cases when the Basic LLM Chain fails to process the chat message properly. It provides a fallback response mechanism to ensure the workflow remains robust.

**Key Features**
- Provides default error messaging
- Maintains consistent response structure
- Connects to the error output branch of the LLM Chain
- Ensures graceful failure handling

The Error Response node activates when the main processing chain encounters issues, ensuring users always receive feedback even when errors occur in the language model processing.

parameters.content:
# 🦙 Ollama Chat Workflow

A simple N8N workflow that integrates Ollama LLM for chat message processing and returns a structured JSON object.

## Overview
This workflow creates a chat interface that processes messages using the Llama 3.2 model through Ollama. When a chat message is received, it gets processed through a basic LLM chain and returns a response.

## Components
- **Trigger Node**
- **Processing Node**
- **Model Node**
- **JSON to Object Node**
- **Structured Response Node**
- **Error Response Node**

## Workflow Structure
1. The chat trigger node receives incoming messages
2. Messages are passed to the Basic LLM Chain
3. The Ollama Model processes the input using Llama 3.2
4. Responses are returned through the chain

## Prerequisites
- N8N installation
- Ollama setup with Llama 3.2 model
- Valid Ollama API credentials

## Configuration
1. Set up the Ollama API credentials in N8N
2. Ensure the Llama 3.2 model is available in your Ollama installation

## Model Node
- Name: Ollama Model
- Type: LangChain Ollama Integration
- Model: llama3.2:latest
- Purpose: Provides the language model capabilities

## Trigger Node
- Name: When chat message received
- Type: Chat Trigger
- Purpose: Initiates the workflow when a new chat message arrives

## Processing Node
- Name: Basic LLM Chain
- Type: LangChain LLM Chain
- Purpose: Handles the processing of messages through the language model and returns a structured JSON object.

### Prompt (Change this for your use case)
Provide the users prompt and response as a JSON object with two fields:
- Prompt
- Response


Avoid any preample or further explanation.
This is the question: {{ $json.chatInput }}

## JSON to Object Node
- Type: Set Node
- Purpose: A node designed to transform and structure response data in a specific format before sending it through the workflow. It operates in manual mapping mode to allow precise control over the response format.

**Key Features**
- Manual field mapping capabilities
- Object transformation and restructuring
- Support for JSON data formatting
- Field-to-field value mapping
- Includes option to add additional input fields

## Structured Response Node
- Type: Set Node
- Purpose: Controls how the workflow responds to users chat prompt.

**Response Mode**
- Manual Mapping: Allows custom formatting of response data
- Fields to Set: Specify which data fields to include in response

## Error Response Node
- Type: Set Node
- Purpose: Handles error cases when the Basic LLM Chain fails to process the chat message properly. It provides a fallback response mechanism to ensure the workflow remains robust.

**Key Features**
- Provides default error messaging
- Maintains consistent response structure
- Connects to the error output branch of the LLM Chain
- Ensures graceful failure handling

The Error Response node activates when the main processing chain encounters issues, ensuring users always receive feedback even when errors occur in the language model processing.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Manual Trigger".
Open the "When chat message received" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Basic LLM Chain" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Ollama Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Structured Response".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Error Response".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "JSON to Object".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
